notes: The Bahamas should score English as fully compatible for travel. Bahamian speech includes local patois and slang, but standard English remains the practical national language for visitors. Bahamas Creole English should also count as a strong local match because it exists in the picker catalog.

evidence.title: Constitution Commission Report: language in The Bahamas
evidence.note: The Constitutional Commission report says English is the primary and perhaps only language of The Bahamas and recommends declaring it the national language.

evidence.title: Bahamas.com slang with translations
evidence.note: Official tourism content explains that Bahamians code-switch between professional English and local slang, which supports English as the default traveler language.

notes: Barbados should score English as fully compatible. Travelers may hear Bajan speech patterns, but English is the national default for public-facing travel use.

evidence.title: Britannica: Barbados
evidence.note: Britannica lists English as Barbados's official language.

evidence.title: Visit Barbados history and culture
evidence.note: Official tourism content says Bajans speak English with a distinct local accent and vocabulary.

notes: Jamaica should score English as fully compatible while acknowledging the strong day-to-day presence of Jamaican Patois. Jamaican Creole English should also count as a minor exact local match because it exists in the picker catalog.

evidence.title: Visit Jamaica language guide
evidence.note: Official tourism content says Jamaica's official language is English and also explains the role of Jamaican Patois.

evidence.title: Visit Jamaica general information
evidence.note: Official tourism FAQs say Jamaica officially speaks English and mixes in island patois.

notes: Turks and Caicos should score English as fully compatible. English is the official traveler-facing language, while Spanish, French, and Turks and Caicos Creole English also appear in community and everyday island use.

evidence.title: Invest Turks and Caicos: businesses in TCI
evidence.note: The official investment portal says English is the official language of the Turks and Caicos Islands, while Creole, French, and Spanish are also spoken in the expatriate community.

evidence.title: Invest Turks and Caicos brochure
evidence.note: The official government brochure lists English as the language of Turks and Caicos.
